text_block_list.name: Sakura Chiyono O
text_block_list.text:
That was so cool, Yaeno! So that's the Kongo
Yaegaki style!

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
Yes. I believe this historic style of martial arts can
be applied to racing, so I commit myself to it
daily.

text_block_list.name: Sakura Chiyono O
text_block_list.text:
That training must be what gives you such great
core strength! Maybe I should try it too.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
Your father was a sumo wrestler, was he not? Did
you ever train with him?

text_block_list.name: Sakura Chiyono O
text_block_list.text:
As a child, yes. I learned how to stomp, use a
teppo pole, slide my feet without raising them,
and do a side split.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
I think those are very valuable experiences,
especially since you've seen the proper
techniques for them many times.

text_block_list.name: Yaeno Muteki
text_block_list.text:
I recommend you try incorporating those
techniques into your training before trying new
ones. You'll be able to train more effectively with
techniques you intimately understand.

text_block_list.name: Sakura Chiyono O
text_block_list.text: Techniques I understand... True, I have watched my dad train hundreds of times before! I practically have those moves memorized!

text_block_list.name: Sakura Chiyono O
text_block_list.text:
I'm going to try it out right away! I bet I'll be able
to understand important points about them that I
didn't realize as a child!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Sakura Chiyono O starts incorporating sumo
training into her schedule and, in doing so,
manages to pick up a few tricks!
